Feb 9, 2000 OmSim ----- OmSim is an environment for modelling and simulation based on Omola, which is an object-oriented language for modelling of continuous time and discrete event dynamical systems. Omola and OmSim are developed as a par

systems. Omola and OmSim are developed as a part of the CACE project at the Deparment of Automatic Control, Lund Institute of Technology, Lund, Sweden. Omola and OmSim Information: http://www.control.lth.se/~cace/omsim.html OmSim on EEC

www.control.lth.se/~cace/omsim.html OmSim on EECS Instructional Systems ----------------------------------- OmSim has been installed for EE291E for our SUN SPARC computers. All users on Instructional UNIX computers can run it with the command

ctional UNIX computers can run it with the command: /home/e/ee291e/omsim/bin/omsim This is a shell script that sets the environment variables and runs the processor-specific binary. It will work on these Instuctional UNIX computers: SU

ork on these Instuctional UNIX computers: SUN login server: c199.eecs.berkeley.edu 199 Cory: all SunRay workstations (SUN) 271/273/275 Soda: all SunRay workstations (SUN) Workstation names are listed in http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/clien

are listed in http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/clients Users can read the on-line documentation with either of these UNIX commands: acroread /home/e/ee291e/omsim/docs/omsim_tutorial.pdf ghostview /home/e/ee291e/omsim/docs/omsim_tutorial.ps netsc

/home/e/ee291e/omsim/docs/omsim_tutorial.ps netscape http://www.control.lth.se/~cace/manual.html OmSim uses XWindows; for help in configuring XWindows on MS Windows and UNIX systems, please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.htm
http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html#xwindows Please report problems to inst@eecs.berkeley.edu. Instructional Support Group 378 & 386 Cory, 333 Soda inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
